'''Epsi Nadir''' was an [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Epsi Collective/Legends|Epsi Collective]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. It was purchased by the [[Arms dealer/Legends|arms dealer]] group known as [[the Most Honourable Guild of Armourers]], which established an [[Guild Auction Hall|auction house]] there. The [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Dej Vennor]] at one point acquired the [[IT-series interrogation droid|interrogation droid]] [[IT-904]] at the auction house.

==Description==
Epsi Nadir was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="GG9" /> located in the [[Epsi Nadir system/Legends|Epsi Nadir system]], a part of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Epsi Collective/Legends|Epsi Collective]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. It shared its name with the sector's [[Epsi Zenith system/Legends|Epsi Zenith system]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
[[File:HonoUrablearmoUrers.png|thumb|right|300px|Epsi Nadir was owned by the Most Honourable Guild of Armourers (an auction of the Guild pictured).]]
At some point, the organization of [[Arms dealer/Legends|weapons dealers]] known as [[the Most Honourable Guild of Armourers]] used its considerable resources to purchase Epsi Nadir in order to bypass interference in its business by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The guild then established there its auction house, the [[Guild Auction Hall]], and acquired legally binding planetary waivers in order to sell [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]]—classified as "[[Collector/Legends|collector's]] items" or "historical artifacts"—to its clients.<ref name="GG9" /> [[Dej Vennor]], a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], who specialized in [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] [[Bounty/Legends|wanted]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and was active by<ref name="TheFarOrbitProject">''[[The Far Orbit Project]]''</ref> [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="TheFarOrbitProjectDate">{{FarOrbitDate}}</ref> purchased the Imperial [[IT-series interrogation droid]] [[IT-904]] from the Guild at an auction on Epsi Nadir.<ref name="TheFarOrbitProject" />

{{RPGseed}}
At one point between 0 [['ABY-BBY' dating system/Legends|ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'' is set in the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates those two battles to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], respectively, and also establishes that the Battle of Yavin was the final event before the beginning of [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].</ref> a group of individuals attended an auction at the Guild Auction Hall, with a number of high-profile bounty hunters also being in attendance.<ref name="GG9" />
{{Endgame}}

==Locations==
The Guild Auction Hall of the Most Honourable Guild of Armourers was located on Epsi Nadir.<ref name="GG9" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:GalaxyGuide9.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Epsi Nadir was first mentioned in ''Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim''.]]
Epsi Nadir was first mentioned in the [[1993]] ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', which was written by [[Simon Smith]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]] for [[West End Games]].<ref name="GG9" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Epsi Nadir [[Star system/Legends|system]], and thereby the planet itself, in grid square M-15.<ref name="TEA" /> In real-world [[Wikipedia:Astronomy|astronomy]], the [[Wikipedia:Nadir|nadir]] is the point on the [[Wikipedia:Celestial sphere|celestial sphere]] that is the opposite of the [[Wikipedia:Zenith|zenith]], or the point directly above the observer.<ref name="Nadir">{{WebCite|url=https://www.britannica.com/science/nadir|text=Nadir | Definition & Astronomy|author=The Editors of Encyclopædia Britannica|date=2018-02-21|work=[[Wikipedia:Encyclopædia Britannica|''Encyclopædia Britannica'']]|archivedate=20251122044245}}</ref>

''Galaxy Guide 9'' includes a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure idea that involves the player characters attending an auction on Epsi Nadir. The book suggests that it be highly dangerous for a [[Lightfight|firefight]] to break out during the event, with such bounty hunters as [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], [[IG-88B/Legends|IG-88]], and [[Zardra]] potentially being present. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="GG9" />
